Details: Oxidation of 17a-ethynyl estradiol (EE2) by chlorine dioxide can be very effective at low CT values and is dependent on the initial contaminant dose, chlorine dioxide dose, contact time and pH. In one study, the reaction of EE2 was too fast to be detected after 5 minutes of contact time with surface water. The study also reported that a dose of 0.1 mg/L was sufficient to oxidize 97% of EE2 in groundwater.
